title: FIFA suspends sanction against US forward Balogun for a year after Trump's call
summary: FIFA's decision to postpone the suspension of the player generates criticism and doubts about the independence of the organization.

title: China launches ballistic missile from nuclear submarine in Pacific and Japan expresses concern
summary: The missile landed outside Japan's exclusive economic zone, but Tokyo sees the action as a serious regional threat.

title: Prime Minister Takaichi agrees to attend budget committee and leaders' debate
summary: After weeks of parliamentary deadlock, the government and opposition agree to resume key sessions.

title: Takaichi's government prioritizes reform of the Imperial House Law amid controversy
summary: The opposition criticizes the government's maneuver to pass the reform without consensus, calling it deceitful and anti-democratic.

title: Father arrested for murder of his two young children in Gunma Prefecture
summary: The bodies of the 6-year-old and 3-year-old children were found with signs of strangulation and the father confessed to the crime.

title: Hayabusa2 probe captures images of asteroid Trifne during successful flyby
summary: JAXA publishes the first photos of the asteroid, which has the shape of a gray snowman, after a high-speed encounter.

title: Man arrested for putting fingers in soy sauce nozzle at Hamazushi
summary: The 43-year-old suspect had previously been fined for a similar act at the same sushi chain.

title: Prime Minister Takaichi embroiled in scandal over defamatory video and his sworn statement
summary: The opposition demands explanations about the alleged involvement of the leader in a video attacking government critics.

title: Japanese actor Koichi Yamamoto dies at 90 from lung cancer
summary: Known for his roles in historical dramas, Yamamoto leaves a great legacy in Japanese film and television.

title: Emperor Naruhito attends Art Academy award ceremony and offers tea to honorees
summary: The emperor honored the artists, including film director Lee Sang-il, for their contribution to Japanese culture.

title: Sapporo and Carlsberg create joint venture in Asia with investment of 102.9 billion yen
summary: The alliance seeks to strengthen beer production and sales in the Asian region amid growing global competition.

title: Impeachment trial begins against Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte for alleged corruption
summary: The process could affect the presidential aspirations of former President Duterte's daughter in the upcoming elections.

title: Russia launches massive drone attack on Kiev, leaving at least 19 dead
summary: The attack occurs on the eve of the NATO summit and exploits the shortage of Ukrainian interceptor missiles.

title: Teenager with arrest warrant escapes by swimming across river and flees in car in Osaka
summary: Police in Aichi were looking for him for an unspecified case; he managed to evade capture for hours.

title: 20-year-old man stabbed in Nagakute, Aichi; attacker remains at large
summary: Police are investigating the incident as an attempted murder and are searching for the suspect who fled the scene.

title: House of Councillors resumes debate while House of Representatives remains stalled
summary: The reform of the Imperial House Law and the 'second capital' project also hinder legislative progress.

title: Hamas announces dissolution of administration in the Gaza Strip
summary: The move leaves the future of government in the Palestinian enclave and negotiations with Israel uncertain.

title: KDDI suffers massive data leak: 12.23 million emails and 7.62 million passwords exposed
summary: The company attributed the breach to an unknown vulnerability and is still investigating the scope of the incident.

title: Japan strengthens expert review to prevent failures in H3 rocket launch
summary: After the launch failure, the Japanese space agency implements corrective measures to avoid new failures.

title: Prime Minister Takaichi says Japan will continuously study strengthening nuclear deterrence
summary: The government does not rule out revising the three non-nuclear principles to adopt a more flexible stance in the face of regional threats.
